title: Agarose gel for RNA and DNA
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"4124i","text":"General method for running agarose gels for RNA and DNA samples","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
steps: 1 g agarose in 100 mL of TAE (Tris-Acetate-EDTA) buffer, 1x Contributed by users
Microwave solution in a flask for 00:03:00 until the agarose is dissolved. Microwave for 00:01:00 thereafter 00:00:30
Allow to cool before adding 1 µL Ethidium bromide, 10mg/mL, 10mL Amresco Catalog #X328-10ML in the gel medium before completely cool. Then pour into the gel mold
Make sure that your plate is balanced and the teeth are in. Decide if you need a long ladder or a short weather.
Add 5 µL Ethidium bromide, 10mg/mL, 10mL Amresco Catalog #X328-10ML in the gel medium within the chromatography chamber

250-300 ng, /RNA per sample C1.V1 = C2.V2 V1 = (10)(50)/RNA conc. (from nanodrop) Add the V1 sample of RNA to 1:1 ratio of RNA Loading Dye (2x) - 4.0 ml New England Biolabs Catalog #B0363S to each sample
Heat up the ladder RiboRuler High Range RNA Ladder Thermo Fisher Catalog #SM1821 and RNA samples to 70 °C for 00:05:00 for the gel
SyberSafe DNA Gel Stain Invitrogen - Thermo Fisher Catalog #S33101 is already in the samples for the PCR; then use the GeneRuler 1 kb Plus DNA Ladder Thermo Fisher Catalog #SM1331
Add 5 µL of ladder and samples to gel wells
For RNA: 80V for 01:00:00 For DNA: 80V for 00:40:00
*Note: can check gel after 00:10:00 to see if there is product showing on the gel
creator: kaylee Beine
